Name: semver-versioning
Steps: Semantic versioning: MAJOR (breaking) / MINOR (feature) / PATCH (fix). Update VERSION constant in main file + CHANGELOG.md + README.md
Pitfall: Never skip CHANGELOG update; version must match across all files
Keywords: version semver major minor patch changelog

Name: changelog-format
Steps: Standard CHANGELOG sections: Added (new features) / Changed (modifications) / Fixed (bug fixes) / Removed (deletions). Date format: YYYY-MM-DD
Command:
## [1.2.3] - 2026-05-23
### Added
- New feature X
### Fixed
- Bug in Y
Pitfall: Keep entries concise; link to issues/PRs if available; newest version at top
Keywords: changelog added changed fixed removed format

Name: git-tag-create
Steps: Create annotated git tag and push
Command: git tag -a v1.2.3 -m 'Release v1.2.3: brief description'\ngit push origin v1.2.3
Pitfall: Use annotated tags (-a) not lightweight; tag format: v + semver; push tags separately
Keywords: git tag annotated push version release

Name: gh-release-create
Pitfall: Always verify assets uploaded correctly; draft releases need manual publish
Keywords: github release create gh cli upload assets

Name: electron-builder-publish
Steps: Build and publish Electron app to GitHub Releases
Command: npx electron-builder --win --publish always\n# Creates DRAFT release — must publish manually on GitHub
Pitfall: electron-builder creates DRAFT releases by default — must go to GitHub and click Publish; test auto-updater after publishing
Keywords: electron builder publish github release draft manual

Name: release-cache-delay
Steps: GitHub CDN caches releases for ~5 minutes
Command: Wait 5 minutes after publishing before testing auto-update. Or restart auto-update service.
Pitfall: Users may get 404 for assets if checking too soon after publish; verify with direct URL
Keywords: release cache delay cdn 5 minutes assets 404

Name: post-release-verify
Steps: Verify release assets and client update. Steps: 1) Check asset list on GitHub 2) Download and test installer 3) Verify auto-updater detects new version 4) Check file sizes match build output
Pitfall: Duplicate releases cause 404 for clients; delete old release before creating new
Keywords: verify release assets test installer auto update

Name: pyinstaller-release
Steps: Build PyInstaller bundle and package for release
Command:
pyinstaller --onedir --name server app.py
# Verify output size
ls -lh dist/server/server.exe  # Should be ~37MB
# Zip for distribution
cd dist && zip -r ../server-win64.zip server/
Pitfall: Always verify exe size matches expectations (too small = missing files; too big = stale build)
Keywords: pyinstaller build release zip package verify size

Name: multi-server-deploy
Command: python server_manager.py deploy [server_name]\npython server_manager.py status\npython server_manager.py logs [server_name] [N_lines]
Pitfall: Deploy one server at a time and verify before continuing; keep rollback plan
Keywords: deploy multi server sync update status logs

Name: version-update-checklist
Steps: Pre-release version update checklist: 1) Update VERSION constant 2) Update CHANGELOG.md 3) Update README.md version badge 4) Commit with message 'chore: bump to vX.Y.Z' 5) Create git tag 6) Push commits + tags
Command: git add -A && git commit -m 'chore: bump to v1.2.3' && git tag -a v1.2.3 -m 'v1.2.3' && git push && git push --tags
Pitfall: Never bump version without changelog entry; commit version bump BEFORE creating tag
Keywords: version update checklist bump commit tag push

Name: hotfix-flow
Steps: Emergency hotfix flow: 1) Branch from latest tag 2) Fix and test 3) Bump PATCH version 4) Merge to main 5) Tag and release
Command: git checkout -b hotfix/v1.2.4 v1.2.3\n# fix the bug\ngit checkout main && git merge hotfix/v1.2.4
Pitfall: Always branch from tag not main; increment PATCH only; merge back to main after
Keywords: hotfix emergency patch branch tag merge

Name: release-notes-template
Command:
## What's New
- Feature A description

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed issue with X (#123)

## Breaking Changes
- API endpoint Y renamed to Z

## Upgrade Notes
- Run 'npm install' to update dependencies
Pitfall: Include Breaking Changes section if any; link to issues; mention upgrade steps
Keywords: release notes template new fixes breaking upgrade
